title: Social Media Marketing
description: Learn how to build a brand and engage with customers on the world's largest social platforms. This course covers organic strategy, community management, and the fundamentals of paid social advertising.

seo_metadata.title: Social Media Marketing Certificate Course | ProCerix
seo_metadata.description: Master Social Media Marketing. Learn organic strategy, community management, content creation, and influencer marketing.

learning_outcomes: Develop a cohesive brand voice for social platforms
Create an engaging organic content calendar
Understand the algorithmic differences between major platforms (Meta, TikTok, LinkedIn)
Implement effective community management and crisis response strategies
Measure social media ROI through engagement and conversion metrics

modules.title: Strategy and Content Creation
modules.description: Building the foundation of your social presence.

lessons.title: Choosing the Right Platforms
lessons.content:
Where is Your Audience?

Not every business needs to be on every platform. B2B companies thrive on LinkedIn, while visually driven B2C brands excel on Instagram and TikTok. Align your platform choice with your buyer personas.

lessons.title: The Content Calendar
lessons.content:
Consistency is Key

A content calendar plans what, when, and where you will post. It ensures a mix of content types (educational, entertaining, promotional) and prevents last-minute scrambling.

modules.title: Community and Influencers
modules.description: Engaging with your audience and leveraging creators.

lessons.title: Community Management
lessons.content:
Two-Way Communication

Social media is not a broadcast channel. Community management involves responding to comments, handling customer service inquiries, and fostering a sense of belonging among your followers.

lessons.title: Influencer Marketing Basics
lessons.content:
Borrowing Trust

Partnering with influencers allows you to reach their highly engaged audiences. Micro-influencers (10k-50k followers) often have higher engagement rates and are more cost-effective than macro-influencers.

lessons.title: Measuring Social Success
lessons.content:
Beyond Vanity Metrics

While Likes and Follows are nice, you must track metrics that impact the business: Engagement Rate, Click-Through Rate to your website, and Conversions.

lessons.title: Organic vs. Paid Social
lessons.content:
Pay to Play

Organic reach on platforms like Facebook is famously low (often under 5%). To guarantee your content is seen by your target audience, you must supplement organic efforts with Paid Social advertising.

mcqs.question: When developing a social media strategy, what is the first step?

options.content: Creating a TikTok account immediately
Identifying where your target audience (buyer personas) actually spends their time
Buying fake followers to look established
Running a massive paid ad campaign

mcqs.question: Which platform is generally considered the most effective for B2B (Business-to-Business) marketing?

options.content: Snapchat
TikTok
LinkedIn
Pinterest

mcqs.question: What is the purpose of a Content Calendar?

options.content: To track employees' vacation days
To plan and organize what, when, and where content will be published
To automatically delete old posts
To track the company's financial budget

mcqs.question: What is 'Community Management' in the context of social media?

options.content: Only posting promotional sales flyers
Actively engaging with followers, responding to comments, and handling customer service
Managing the physical office space
Blocking anyone who asks a question

mcqs.question: Why do many brands prefer working with 'micro-influencers' over celebrities?

options.content: Micro-influencers have billions of followers
Micro-influencers often have higher engagement rates and are more cost-effective
Micro-influencers do not use the internet
Micro-influencers own the social platforms

mcqs.question: Which of the following is considered a 'vanity metric'?

options.content: Click-Through Rate (CTR)
Total Follower Count
Conversion Rate
Cost Per Acquisition (CPA)
The number of people who see your content without you paying for distribution
The number of people who clicked a sponsored ad
Marketing campaigns focused solely on organic food
The total number of employees in a marketing department

mcqs.question: Why has Paid Social become necessary for many brands on platforms like Facebook?

options.content: Because Facebook charges a fee just to create a business page
Because organic reach for business pages has declined significantly over the years
Because it is illegal not to pay
Because nobody uses Facebook anymore

mcqs.question: Engagement Rate is typically calculated by:

options.content: Dividing total sales by follower count
Dividing total interactions (likes, comments, shares) by total reach or followers
Subtracting unfollows from new followers
Counting the number of posts per week

mcqs.question: What is a 'Call to Action' (CTA) on a social media post?

options.content: The legal disclaimer at the bottom
A direct instruction to the audience, such as 'Click the link in bio' or 'Sign up today'
The copyright symbol
A complaint made by a customer
